tasks.title: Fix HTTP 500 login page — Turbopack IIFE bug with NEXT_PUBLIC_* env var inlining
tasks.description: Login page returns HTTP 500 because Turbopack inlines NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY into the client chunk module factory, generating a broken IIFE where string values are passed as the module context parameter (e.i is not a function). Fix: restructure lib/supabase/client.ts to use dynamic import() of @supabase/ssr, which forces a separate chunk boundary and prevents the IIFE collision. Update all 11 files / 13 callers to await the now-async createClient().

tasks.title: DevOps verification — Supabase migration + Cloudflare Pages setup
tasks.description: Verify all config files for the Supabase + Cloudflare migration: wrangler.toml, open-next.config.ts, next.config.mjs, middleware.ts (renamed from proxy.ts — see MZ-013), supabase/migrations/, env files, build pipeline, supabase client config. Outcome: config files verified; P1/P2 fixes done (MZ-006..MZ-009); 12 pre-existing TS errors fixed; cf:build green; cf:preview verified (worker served 200/307 on /, /en, /en/login, /api/ping); Windows CI green via MZ-012 run #1 (windows-latest 7m 46s). The QA plan Section 1 (schema migration replay against a fresh Supabase project, RLS policy spot-checks) is intentionally out-of-scope for this task — it requires standing up a sacrificial Supabase project and is tracked separately as future work.

tasks.title: QA plan — Supabase migration + Cloudflare Pages setup

tasks.title: Fix P1#1 — audit-logs route.ts TS cast (double cast via unknown)
tasks.description: Fix TypeScript error at app/api/audit-logs/route.ts:21 — row as Record<string, unknown> fails because Papa Parse data can be ParserError. Cast via unknown first.

tasks.title: Fix P1#2 — proxy.ts session cookie detection for Supabase SSR
tasks.description: proxy.ts:77 checks for cookie named 'session' but Supabase SSR uses sb-*-auth-token cookies. Fix session detection to use Supabase SSR cookie pattern.

tasks.title: Deploy Google Drive upload integration to dev
tasks.description: Land the Drive upload feature (already committed in 4fee262) to dev.makhzoon.me. Steps: (1) Apply supabase/migrations/0007_avatar.sql against the shared Supabase project via SQL Editor — NOTE: shared DB affects dev/staging/prod simultaneously. (2) Set three Worker secrets per env via `npx wrangler secret put` — GOOGLE_DRIVE_CLIENT_EMAIL, GOOGLE_DRIVE_PRIVATE_KEY, GOOGLE_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID (source from .env.local). (3) Run `npm run cf:deploy -- --env dev`. (4) Smoke test avatar upload at dev.makhzoon.me/<locale>/<orgSlug>/profile. (5) Repeat secrets + merge to STGBranch/main when ready. Drive folder: 1y6VunC5Qc0_t7MO5tF_b26eg_VN43Exq (service account has Editor).

tasks.title: Decide P3 #5 — OpenNext Windows compatibility warning
tasks.description: Resolution chosen 2026-05-18: do not accept-and-document (colleague builds on Windows daily). Instead, added .github/workflows/build.yml — matrix of ubuntu-latest + windows-latest running `npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run cf:build && bundle-size check` on pushes to DevBranch/STGBranch/main and on pull_request. Outcome: workflow committed in e982de5 and pushed; GitHub Actions run #1 (build.yml on commit e982de5) completed green — Build (ubuntu-latest) 2m 32s, Build (windows-latest) 7m 46s. Windows builds are now continuously verified on every push and PR; regressions will be caught automatically. Cosmetic annotations: actions/checkout@v4 uses Node 20 (deprecated, awaiting v5); GitHub will redirect windows-latest to windows-2025-vs2026 by 2026-06-15 (automatic).

tasks.title: Rename proxy.ts → middleware.ts (Edge runtime for OpenNext CF Workers)
tasks.description: Incidental fix surfaced during MZ-004 build verification. Next.js 16 introduced `proxy.ts` as an alternative middleware file that defaults to Node.js runtime; @opennextjs/cloudflare 1.19.10 does not yet support Node middleware and aborts with 'Node.js middleware is not currently supported. Consider switching to Edge Middleware.' Fix: renamed proxy.ts → middleware.ts (legacy convention, always Edge runtime), renamed exported function `proxy` → `middleware`. No behavior change — same routing logic, just runs on Edge. The matcher config and all branching preserved. Verified: ubuntu + windows matrix run #1 both green on commit e982de5; cf:preview locally served 307 redirect on /en (the middleware's auth gate fires correctly). Colleague should `git pull` before his next local build.

tasks.title: Remove legacy Firebase GitHub Actions workflows from main
tasks.description: Background: commit e70811e (refactor: remove legacy seeding scripts and storage rules) deleted .github/workflows/deploy-mirror-functions.yml and .github/workflows/sync-firestore.yml from DevBranch. These files were already deleted from main (verified — both paths return 'does not exist in origin/main'). No action needed.
